Title: Chitin and chitosan remodeling defines vegetative development and Trichoderma biocontrol

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Chitin synthase exomer adaptor homolog of ScChs5
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports proper wall assembly, growth and stress resistance
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion reduced growth, lowered cell-wall chitin, increased branching and caused strong CFW sensitivity, consistent with defective chitin synthase trafficking
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Homolog identification; targeted deletion; growth assay; microscopy; chitin quantification
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. cse5 is required for normal growth and wall integrity. 2. Δcse5 displays laminar, highly branched mycelium and reduced chitin. 3. cse5 likely supports export/trafficking of one or more chitin synthases.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to optimize secretion of wall-synthesis enzymes in biocontrol fungi
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phenotype stronger than Δchs4, suggesting effects on additional chitin synthases beyond CHS4.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Chitin synthase export chaperone homolog of ScChs7/NcCSE7
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports proper synthase export, wall integrity and growth
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion reduced growth, caused branching defects and increased sensitivity to cell-wall stress, indicating defective chitin synthase export
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Homolog identification; targeted deletion; growth assay; microscopy; chitin quantification
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. cse7 contributes to normal growth and wall robustness. 2. Δcse7 shows strongly branched laminar mycelium and CFW sensitivity. 3. cse7 likely mediates export of CHS proteins from the ER.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Perturbation impairs cell-wall assembly and fitness
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A useful accessory target to improve wall-synthesis efficiency if properly modulated
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper suggests it may influence secretion of other chitin synthases, not only CHS4.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Host and self cell walls during interaction
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall remodeling, mobilization of chitosan during mycoparasitism and self-recognition
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Moderately to strongly induced during self-contact and especially after host contact
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strong upregulation during mycoparasitism, especially against Rhizoctonia solani after contact
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: These chitosanases were highly induced during host invasion, indicating roles in chitosan mobilization from host and/or self cell walls
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic identification; qRT-PCR during confrontation assays
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Six chitosanases were identified in T. atroviride. 2. cho1-3 and cho5 are strongly induced during late mycoparasitic stages, particularly against R. solani. 3. They likely contribute to host-wall degradation and self/non-self wall remodeling.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Not functionally deleted here, so tradeoffs not established
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising enzyme set for improving biocontrol penetration and host range
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core genes emphasized in mycoparasitism results, but direct knockout validation was not reported in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fungal cell wall, especially hyphal tips and septa
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall integrity, vegetative development, conidiation, mycoparasitic attack, stress protection
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Chitin biosynthesis / fungal cell wall remodeling
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Polymerizes UDP-N-acetyl-D-glucosamine into β-1,4-linked chitin chains
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plasma membrane / hyphal tip-septum cell wall synthesis sites
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed during hyphal development and conidiation at intermediate level
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed throughout hyphal development; still expressed in mature conidia; not notably induced by tested stresses; moderately induced during mycoparasitism
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Vegetative growth; conidiation; cell wall stress dyes; osmotic stress; oxidative stress; mycoparasitic confrontation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion reduced growth rate, reduced spore production, altered branching and thinner hyphae, reduced cell wall chitin, and strongly impaired mycoparasitism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Targeted knockout; complementation; qRT-PCR; CFW/CR microscopy; GlcNAc cell-wall assay; confrontation assays
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step for vegetative development, not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. chs1 is required for normal hyphal growth, conidiation and cell wall chitin deposition. 2. Δchs1 strains show strong sensitivity to cell wall stress and near-avirulence at host contact. 3. chs1 contributes to chitin-based wall integrity during vegetative growth and mycoparasitism.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion causes severe developmental defects and reduced biocontrol fitness
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target to modulate fungal cell wall robustness and biocontrol performance, but disruption strongly compromises fitness
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of five non-redundant chitin synthases highlighted as indispensable for mycoparasitism.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall integrity, stress adaptation, development, mycoparasitism
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Chitin biosynthesis / fungal cell wall remodeling
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Polymerizes UDP-N-acetyl-D-glucosamine into β-1,4-linked chitin chains
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plasma membrane / cell wall synthesis sites
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed during hyphal development; decreases during conidial maturation but remains detectable
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High during vegetative growth; strongest responder among CHS to several cell wall disturbing agents and harsh treatments; not significantly altered during mycoparasitism
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: CWSD stress, high glucose/SDS, ROS, vegetative growth, confrontation assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion reduced growth and spore formation, altered branching and increased tip/lateral wall staining, caused strong dye sensitivity and compromised mycoparasitism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Knockout; qRT-PCR stress profiling; microscopy; growth assays; confrontation assays
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step for stress-compensatory chitin synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. chs2 is highly expressed during vegetative growth and responds to cell wall stress. 2. Δchs2 shows reduced growth, reduced sporulation and altered chitin deposition. 3. chs2 likely mediates compensatory wall synthesis under stress.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss reduces fungal growth and antagonistic performance
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could be leveraged to enhance stress robustness of biocontrol strains, but requires preserving normal development
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Deletion did not markedly reduce bulk cell-wall chitin under tested condition, indicating functional specialization beyond total chitin amount.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall construction during vegetative development and conidiation
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Chitin biosynthesis / fungal cell wall remodeling
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Polymerizes UDP-N-acetyl-D-glucosamine into β-1,4-linked chitin chains
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plasma membrane / cell wall synthesis sites
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High during vegetative development; remains expressed in mature conidia
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High during hyphal development; not induced by tested stresses; not significantly altered during mycoparasitism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion reduced cell-wall chitin and contributed to developmental defects; implicated as important for vegetative development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Knockout; qRT-PCR; chitin quantification; growth/conidiation assays
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key developmental contributor, rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. chs3 is highly expressed during development. 2. Loss of chs3 lowers cell-wall chitin and contributes to growth/conidiation defects. 3. chs3 acts with cda2 as a constitutive developmental module.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Disruption weakens development and likely lowers biocontrol efficiency
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for understanding basal wall synthesis, but unsuitable for disruption in applied strains
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as tandem candidate with cda2 for maintaining basal deacetylation/chitin production.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Myosin motor domain-containing chitin synthase with Dek domain
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fungal cell wall, polarized growth sites, septa
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Polarized growth, branching, septation, conidiation, cell wall integrity, mycoparasitism
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Chitin biosynthesis / fungal cell wall remodeling
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: MMD-containing class V chitin synthase branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Synthesizes chitin at sites requiring polarized growth and specialized wall assembly
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed throughout hyphal development, vegetative growth and conidial maturation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High constitutive developmental expression; moderately induced by cell wall disturbance and during mycoparasitism; promoter enriched in developmental and CWI elements
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Development; CFW/CR stress; ROS; host confrontation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion caused severe growth defects, irregular colonies, little/no conidial production, strong cell-wall stress sensitivity, major chitin reduction, abnormal septation and near loss of mycoparasitic attack
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Knockout; complementation; promoter analysis; microscopy; chitin assay; confrontation assay
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: major key control step for polarized chitin deposition and virulence-related growth
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. chs5 is one of the most critical chitin synthases for vegetative development and conidiation. 2. Δchs5 has severely reduced chitin content, aberrant polarized growth and strong mycoparasitic defects. 3. chs5 is a non-redundant MMD-CHS essential for biocontrol-related fungal development.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss is nearly lethal developmentally and abolishes desired biocontrol traits
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value engineering target for improving wall robustness/biocontrol if optimized rather than disrupted; caution due to strong pleiotropy
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Forms a head-to-head genomic arrangement with chs7; strongest developmental importance among core genes.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Myosin motor domain-containing chitin synthase with Dek domain
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fungal cell wall, polarized growth sites, septa
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Polarized growth, conidiation, wall integrity, mycoparasitism
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Chitin biosynthesis / fungal cell wall remodeling
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: MMD-containing class VII chitin synthase branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Synthesizes chitin required for polarized growth and proper septation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed throughout hyphal development and conidial maturation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High developmental expression; induced by cell wall stress and mycoparasitism
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Development; CWSD stress; ROS; host confrontation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion severely impaired growth, conidiation and mycoparasitism, caused patchy chitin deposition, hyperseptation, strong cell-wall dye sensitivity and the lowest cell-wall chitin among mutants
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Knockout; qRT-PCR; microscopy; chitin assay; confrontation assay
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: major key control step for polarized chitin synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. chs7 is highly expressed during development and induced during mycoparasitism. 2. Δchs7 causes severe structural and developmental defects with drastic chitin loss. 3. chs7 is indispensable for effective host invasion and biocontrol.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Disruption causes extreme developmental and virulence-associated defects
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important leverage point for strengthening mycoparasitic performance if finely tuned; not suitable for knockout
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Together with chs5 defines the most severe phenotypes among synthase mutants.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Atypical class VI-related chitin/hyaluronan synthase-like protein with QRKRW motif
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fungal cell wall during host interaction
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Chitin biosynthesis / specialized cell wall remodeling
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely synthesizes a specialized chitin-related glycopolymer during stress and mycoparasitism
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced by osmotic stress, ROS, and mycoparasitic confrontation with all hosts; weak in normal growth
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Osmotic stress; ROS; mycoparasitic host interaction
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion had mild vegetative effects but altered branching and reduced mycoparasitic competence; strongly implicated in host-interaction-specific wall remodeling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression profiling; knockout; microscopy; confrontation assays
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: condition-dependent key control step during mycoparasitism
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. chs8 is a stress- and mycoparasitism-induced atypical chitin synthase. 2. It is co-induced with cda1 and likely participates in a protective cell-wall remodeling program. 3. Δchs8 shows altered host-interaction morphology, supporting a specialized virulence role.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulation may alter stress resilience and host interaction specificity
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to improve host-interaction fitness of biocontrol strains under stress
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper discusses possible hyaluronan-synthase-like dual substrate capacity, but this remains speculative.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: CE4 chitin deacetylase with signal peptide and CBM18 chitin-binding motifs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fungal cell wall during mycoparasitic interaction
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall plasticity, resistance to host chitinases, ROS protection, mycoparasitic virulence
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Removes acetyl groups from chitin to generate chitosan-like wall polymers
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced by osmotic stress, ROS, and all mycoparasitic confrontations; low in normal development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion caused severe defects in host overgrowth, growth arrest at contact, increased susceptibility to host metabolites and oxidative stress, while triggering compensatory lytic-enzyme induction
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Knockout; qRT-PCR; confrontation assay; microscopy; oxidative-stress and exudate-sensitivity assays
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step for virulence-associated chitosan formation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. cda1 is one of the strongest induced genes during mycoparasitism and ROS stress. 2. Δcda1 is severely defective in host attack and stress resistance. 3. cda1 likely generates protective deacetylated wall polymers crucial for biocontrol.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Disruption reduces virulence against target fungi and increases environmental susceptibility
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-priority target for enhancing biocontrol robustness and host invasion, but should be increased or optimized rather than removed
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Co-induced with chs8 from the same genomic neighborhood; phylogenetically linked to virulence-related CDAs.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Membrane-associated CE4 chitin deacetylase with N-terminal transmembrane helix
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Cell wall / membrane-associated nascent wall region
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Vegetative development, apical dominance, cell wall plasticity
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deacetylates cell-wall-associated nascent chitin during vegetative growth
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Intermediate expression during vegetative growth and early conidiation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutively expressed during hyphal development; not induced by ROS; not significantly altered during mycoparasitism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion caused early growth suppression, reduced sporulation, dysregulated apical dominance, altered tip chitin deposition and increased sensitivity to host-related stresses
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Knockout; qRT-PCR; microscopy; growth and sporulation assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. cda2 is a constitutively expressed membrane-associated deacetylase important for vegetative development. 2. Δcda2 shows abnormal branching/apical dominance and reduced conidiation. 3. cda2 likely acts with constitutive chitin synthesis to maintain basal wall deacetylation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to modulate hyphal growth form in applied strains, but with risk to development
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Only CDA with predicted transmembrane helix in T. atroviride; exception among CDAs in not being ROS-induced.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Degenerate CE4-like chitin deacetylase domain lacking conserved catalytic residues
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Conidial maturation and mycoparasitism-associated wall remodeling
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Putative noncanonical chitin deacetylase-like role in wall remodeling rather than confirmed catalysis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Very weak in hyphal growth but strongly induced during conidial maturation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by osmotic stress, ROS, self-recognition and strongly during host overgrowth
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Conidial maturation; HOG; ROS; self/non-self interaction; mycoparasitism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion reduced spore production and caused sustained induction of lytic indicator genes during mycoparasitism, supporting a critical role in host interaction and spore-related wall maturation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Knockout; expression profiling; sporulation assay; confrontation assay
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: condition-dependent key control component, catalytic status not established
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. cda3 is specifically linked to conidial maturation and mycoparasitism rather than basal vegetative growth. 2. Δcda3 reduces conidiation and alters mycoparasitic transcriptional responses. 3. cda3 may act as a noncanonical regulatory remodeling protein.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulation may disturb sporulation and host-response tuning
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Interesting target for controlling sporulation and host-attack programs, but function is unusual and requires caution
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion suggests it may have evolved as a regulatory protein rather than an active enzyme.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Vegetative hyphae and early conidial stages
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall flexibility, conidial maturation, oxidative-stress protection, mycoparasitism
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deacetylates wall chitin contributing to cell wall flexibility and maturation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High during vegetative growth and early conidial maturation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by osmotic stress, harsh cell wall stress, ROS, and self-recognition
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Vegetative growth; conidiation; ROS; HOG; self-recognition
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion caused altered conidial morphology, reduced cell-wall chitin, enhanced oxidative-stress susceptibility and reduced mycoparasitic performance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Knockout; qRT-PCR; microscopy; GlcNAc assay; confrontation assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. cda6 is highly expressed during development and stress responses. 2. Δcda6 alters conidial properties and lowers chitin-related wall measurements. 3. cda6 contributes to wall maturation and stress protection relevant to biocontrol.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Perturbation can alter sporulation quality and environmental resilience
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential tuning target for spore quality and stress resilience in biocontrol formulations
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highly homologous to CDA4, suggesting partial duplication-related specialization.
